Incident Type: Fatal incident of a contract employee
What Happened: : Deceased Person (DP) Mr. Behera was a pillion rider on a two-wheeler, going from HSM ( Hot Strip Mill) to SMS-1 (Steel Melting Shop) at his workplace. Ahead of them, a coal tar tanker under IBMD (Industrial By- Product Management Department) was going in the same direction. While the tanker took l
Preliminary Findings: 1. Deceased person was the pillion. 2. Both were wearing Crash Helmet 3. Both were going for their job of pasting stickers on HR (Hot Rolled) coil from HSM to SMS-1. 4. Tanker belongs to a customer who had come to collect the tar from coke oven under IBMD division. 5. CCTV footage captured about 50 m before the site of accident reveals both the persons on bike were talking on mobile while riding.
Recommendations: Not Found

Incident Type: Fatal incident of a contract employee
Injury Description: Internal injury in the lower abdomen
What Happened: : M/s Offshore Infrastructures Limited was engaged at the CRM Project site for mechanical and piping work. On 8th March 2022, their working team finished work alongside the entry bay area of the Continuous Galvanizing Line and post-closure of the work permit, they were cleaning the area. An F-15 (pi
Preliminary Findings: 1. Standard Work Procedure (SWP) was available for the job. Mr Sadanand Behera and the team had completed the day’s work, and the work permit had been returned. 2. F15 crane operator was doing the reverse movement on a downward sloping road. 3. F15 crane operator joined TSK site in January 2022 and has around 5 years of experience.
Recommendations: Not Found

Injury Description: Fracture of fifth metacarpal bone of left hand
What Happened: : On 11th March 2022, the injured person (IP) was engaged in PR-01 pump rotor installation job at ‘C’ Furnace Cooling Plant Pump House. While moving another side of the equipment, the IP jumped over the suction pipe of 500 mm diameter instead of using a cross over bridge provided for the same. In th
Preliminary Findings: 1. SOP for the job was available. 2. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate. 3. IP was using all required PPEs.
Recommendations: 1. Communication to all concerned employees i. No shortcut should be taken while performing any activities. ii. Always use designated pathway/crossover/staircase for the movement. iii. More emphasis to be put on the reporting of the incident to the concerned personnel. 2. Emphasis on improving the appropriate behaviour at the workplace through a toolbox talk.

Injury Description: Left foot – crush injury with amputation of toes
What Happened: : On 5th March 2022, the injured person (IP) was on A-shift duty. Goliath Crane # 2 was moving towards the plate storage area for lifting the plate, which was supposed to be loaded on the Proarc cutting machine. Suddenly the crane operator stopped the crane after hearing a loud yelling sound. The op
Preliminary Findings: 1. The left foot of IP got entrapped between the Goliath crane wheel and rail. 2. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate. 3. IP was using safety shoe.
Recommendations: 1. Hard barricade should be provided on both sides of the rail throughout the length. 2. Optical sensor to be installed on both sides of each leg of Goliath Crane. 3. Wheel guard to be installed.

What Happened: : On 9th March 2022, the injured person was in on-job training and going downstairs to inspect the coil bottom surface at the Coil dividing line. While going downwards on the stair, he slipped. As a result, his left leg got twisted. He informed his superiors about the incident at around 10 am and su
Preliminary Findings: 1. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate. 2. IP was using all required PPEs. 3. Staircase was found free from Oil & Grease.
Recommendations: 1. Employees should be attentive while using stairs. 2. High-resolution camera to be installed for coil inspection.

What Happened: : On 18th Jan 2022, the injured person (IP) was going home after completing 'C' shift duty. While he was crossing the road via zebra crossing near SBI ATM, suddenly a company employee coming to join ‘A’ shift duty on his two-wheeler hit him. As a result, he sustained an injury to his right leg. Imme
Preliminary Findings: 1. Road was in good condition. 2. There was foggy weather at the time of the incident. 3. It seems that the two-wheeler employee was in hurry to do In-Punch.
Recommendations: 1. All employees should be attentive while driving vehicle inside the workplace and follow the road safety guideline.

What Happened: : On 15th Jan 2022, West Bokaro rake unloading activity was going on at CWT#2. After completing the unloading of the 25th wagon. It was being pushed towards outhaul side by Side Arm Charger to couple it with the empty wagons. During this activity, a jerk was generated. As a result, one of the defect
Preliminary Findings: 1. Wagon door was not in good condition also the door latch was found defective. 2. SOP mentions maintaining a safe distance from moving wagons. 3. IP was using all required PPEs. 4. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate.
Recommendations: 1. Side Barricading to be shifted away to ensure a maximum safe distance from moving wagons. 2. Mesh to be provided to prevent any body part exposure towards moving wagon beyond the barricading.

Injury Description: Hairline fracture in 5th metatarsal bone of left foot
What Happened: On 29th December 2021, after completing shift duty, injured person (IP) was going towards the attendance punching machine for punch out. While descending through the staircase near Pre-treatment Plant (PTP), IP felt a slight sprain in his left foot. In X-ray, hairline fracture was detected in the 5th metatarsal bone of the left foot. Risk Type : Blue Risk (C3, L3) Last date of Incident (LTI) of department : 29.03.2021 Incident (LTI) free days : 275 Days Under camera surveillance : No
Preliminary Findings: 1. IP was using all required PPEs. 2. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate. 3. Staircase was having adequate handrails and toe guards.
Recommendations: 1. Behavioural safety training on attentiveness to be given to all concerned employees.

Injury Description: Cut injury in Right-hand index finger.
What Happened: : On 22nd December 2021, the injured person (IP) was deployed in EX01 excavator at VI OB Sec D. A problem in a ladder operation (not working smoothly) of the excavator was reported by IP at around 05:30 PM. This problem was attended by OEM (Tata Hitachi) and rectified. The equipment was operated by
Preliminary Findings: 1. Excavator Ladder was found stuck at the end of shift. 2. SOP was not followed. (Did not inform the concerned Supervisor about the abnormality in the ladder)
Recommendations: 1. Ladder (other than spring loaded) to be explored and replaced in Ex01 and other Ex-2500 excavators in consultation with OEM.

Injury Description: Pain in right hand wrist joint
What Happened: : On 1st December 2021, the injured person (IP) was coming to attend B-shift duty on his two-wheeler. While reaching the MHS Punching machine area of LD#2 & SC, as he took a left turn his two-wheeler skidded on a small piece of slag debris lying on the road. As a result, he fell on the road and sust
Preliminary Findings: 1. Condition of the Road was found good, but a few small pieces of slag were lying on the road. 2. IP was wearing a crash helmet at the time of incident.
Recommendations: 1. Counselling to the concerned employee to be done at the departmental level for Safe Driving. 2. Regular cleaning of the road needs to be ensured.

Injury Description: Partial amputation of right leg foot
What Happened: : On 28th November 2021, the injured person (IP) was doing routine checking of material flow through the inspection door of Rotary Air seal valve – 2 (RAV-2). However, it appears that he did not follow the SOP and did not maintain the safe distance. As a result, his right foot got trapped in RAV and
Preliminary Findings: 1. The IP was using all required PPEs. 2. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate.
Recommendations: 1. Grating should be provided in between moving blades and the inspection cover to eliminate the risk of material getting stuck up. 2. Identification of all manual activity in the hazardous area of TSK O&M and elimination of these activities through digital/engineering controls.

Incident Type: Fatal incident of a contract employee
What Happened: : On 26th Oct 2021, the deceased person (DP) was engaged as a flagman to guide and position the dumper carrying soil for refilling. After positioning the dumper for unloading of soil, he stood near the pipes stacked on the ground for project work. While the dumper was getting unloaded, he moved back
Preliminary Findings: 1. DP was standing on the left side of the dumper after positioning. 2. Construction pipes were kept at the site without proper barricading and signage. 3. Illumination at the incident site was found inadequate.
Recommendations: 1. Reinforce training on Six directional hazards among the workforce. 2. Backfilling and soil dumping activities to be avoided after sunset/at night. 3. Areas where material is kept for project construction is to be barricaded properly.

Injury Description: Fracture of phalanx of right hand little finger
What Happened: : On 21st Oct 2021, the injured person (IP) along with his colleague was deputed for the troubleshooting job of the air compressor in the general shift. the positive isolation was ensured at the site. IP was trying to pull the compressor belt. In this process, his right-hand little finger got entang
Preliminary Findings: 1. The IP is having more than 30 years of work experience. 2. The process of manually rectifying the compressor belt and pulley is not mentioned in the existing SOP. (SOP No- QEHSMS/446/P3370 NIM) 3. Illumination at the incident site was found inadequate. 4. The IP was not using hand gloves during the time of the incident.
Recommendations: 1. SOP for operation of air compressor (ELGI make) should be revised and the troubleshooting procedure for attending V-belt jamming should be included in it. 2. Training shall be imparted to concerned employees regarding commonly accepted unsafe practices (CAUP). 3. Guarding of V-belt should be done in such a way that, without removing the guard, no one can touch it.

Incident Type: Fatal incident of a contract employee
Injury Description: Damaged chest with multiple fractures at right arm and leg
What Happened: : On 26th October 2021, a preventive maintenance contractor team comprising of one supervisor and four workmen went up to the steel plant rooftop area for lubricating the canopy duct damper mechanism and inspection of the expansion joint. After finishing the job of 3rd damper with a safety harness a
Preliminary Findings: 1. Deceased person was working with M/s Nessman for last 1.5 months. However, he has worked at other companies for several years. 2. All team members were trained on the required safety standard and passed the health check-up requirements for working at height. 3. All were using the required PPEs. 4. The rusted metal sheets in that area are more than 5 years old. It’s a no-man land. There was a plan to repair the same because of water leakage during rains.
Recommendations: Not Found

What Happened: : On 05th Oct 2021, the injured person (IP) was engaged in the unloading of pipe fittings materials. There was a stack of 36 blind flanges along with other materials on the truck bed. Out of 36 blinds flanges, 24 blind flanges were already unloaded. During making space to provide wooden dunnage belo
Preliminary Findings: 1. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate. 2. SWP was available for the job. 3. Work permit was taken for the job. 4. The stack of blind flanges was kept in unstable condition on the vehicle. 5. The surface of the material was found greasy. 6. IP was using all required PPEs. 7. Weight of a single piece of the flange was approx. 242 Kg.
Recommendations: 1. Proper packing and unitization for loose materials to be followed during supply and transportation. 2. Workers need to be more vigilant during loading and unloading activities.

What Happened: : On 9th September 2021, the injured person (IP) was deputed in LC-19 conveyor as a belt attender to fill the product bin of kiln1-7. At around 8:05 pm, while IP was going for checking the product bin level at LC-19 conveyor gallery, his right leg got twisted on the cross over made over LC-19 rail t
Preliminary Findings: 1. Handrail of staircase was found in good condition. 2. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate.
Recommendations: 1. All employees should use handrails while walking on the staircase and a three-point contact rule must be followed.

What Happened: On 29th September 2021, the injured person (IP) was going for lunch from TSCR to her residence. On the way near Merchant Mill office, while IP was passing through the level crossing, her 2-wheeler got skidded. As a result, IP fell on the road resulting in an injury to her left knee. IP was immediate
Preliminary Findings: 1. When IP reached the level crossing, she started moving towards the right side of the road. In this process, she came parallel to the rail track. 2. IP was driving the vehicle within the specified speed limit. 3. Road condition was found good. 4. Rail track is crossing the road at an angle which is creating such a skidding hazard. 5. Tyre condition of the vehicle was found good.
Recommendations: 1. Communication on ‘how to cross the level crossing safely’ to be organised. 2. For new joiners, safe driving techniques are to be imparted during the induction phase itself. 3. Explore the feasibility of providing rubberized level crossing at critical locations.

What Happened: : On 13th September 2021, the injured person (IP) was in ‘C’ shift duty. He went to inspect the Euclid Dumper No - 137 which was parked near LD#3 scrap dumping area. After inspection, while he was getting down from the dumper, he became unbalanced resulting in an injury to his left ankle. IP was imm
Preliminary Findings: 1. IP was following the three-point contacts rule while getting down from the vehicle. 2. Floor condition at the incident site was found unsafe. 3. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate.
Recommendations: 1. Inspection of the vehicle should be conducted at the designated place of the customer site. 2. All employees should be attentive while getting up/down from the vehicle.

What Happened: : On 8th September 2021, the injured person (IP) was returning home after completing his duty. Near coke plant main road and battery#3 corner, his bike got skidded. As a result, he fell on the road and sustained an injury. IP felt pain in his left-hand thumb and visited TMH emergency at around 11:15
Preliminary Findings: 1. IP was using crash helmet. 2. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate.
Recommendations: 1. All employees should follow the Safe Driving techniques, slowing down the vehicle especially at islands, near check houses etc. 2. Promoting employees to fill their 2-wheeler fitness declaration form.

What Happened: : On 11th Sept 2021, the injured person (IP) was deployed at 2.5 post with a dog, sustained an injury to his right leg while he was patrolling at the fabrication yard. He was taken first to the MGM hospital emergency by the shift security supervisor, but later shifted to TMH emergency where first ai
Preliminary Findings: 1. IP was sitting outside his duty post in an unsafe place. 2. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate. 3. he was sitting on a chair by keeping his feet near the rails of the crane and was unaware of the crane movement.
Recommendations: 1. Safe patrolling path to be developed as well as scraps lying near perimeter wall to be removed. 2. A system of taking a situation report from MDFY & Project site in every 2 hours should be developed and its record to be maintained by General Duty - TGS Control room in charge.

What Happened: : On 20th Sept 2021, the injured person (IP) was engaged in the scrapping of a heavy rack made of 50mm MS angle. IP was trying to lift this rack along with three other persons to place the wooden packing below the rack for gas cutting. They lifted the one side of the rack manually up to a height of
Preliminary Findings: 1. Manual material handling was done even if the rack approximate weight is around 400kg. 2. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate. 3. Material Handling SOP was available at site.
Recommendations: 1. Material handling should not be carried out manually if the weight of the material is more than 25kg.

Injury Description: Cut injury in left hand index finger
What Happened: : On 31st August 2021, M/s Tata Steel Utilities and Infrastructure Services Ltd (TSUISL) was engaged in civil works of the Air Separation Unit. The injured person (IP) was preparing shuttering boards by using a ply cutting machine. A small wooden piece was stuck in the machine. While the IP was tryi
Preliminary Findings: 1. IP was using all required PPEs. 2. Cutting machine was found in good condition.
Recommendations: 1. Do’s & Don’ts for power tools and equipment usage needs to be strengthened. 2. Site supervision to be strengthened.

Incident Type: Fatal incident of a contract employee
What Happened: : On 25th August 21, erection of rafter beam in JH-21 junction house under Pellet Plant project was planned, as a part of ongoing industrial structure erection work at Kalinganagar. Accordingly work permit (ID:174531) was issued to M/s. iRISE. After tool box talk, a rigger cum operator, Deceased per
Preliminary Findings: 1. Deceased person was positioning manlifter at work site. 2. He had 4 years of working experience on construction site, prior to coming to Tata Steel Kalinganagar. 3. He was certified operator by International Powered Access Federation (IPAF). 4. This was planned job and work permit was issued in i3M system (Integrated Man, Machine & Materials). 5. Illumination at site was in good condition (day light). 6. The control of the manlifter was with the DP himself.
Recommendations: Not Found

Department: Iron Making Mechanical Maintenance (H – Blast Furnace)
What Happened: : On 14th July 2021, the injured person (IP) was engaged in INBA#1 scrap (slow down box) dismantling work. Dismantling of scrap was being done using gas cutting. IP was standing beside the slowdown box during gas cutting job. In the meantime, one portion of slowdown box got toppled on IP’s right foo
Preliminary Findings: 1. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate. 2. Work permit was taken for the job. 3. SOP for the job was available. 4. IP was using all required PPEs.
Recommendations: 1. Strengthen the system of hazard identification and risk mitigation before starting the job. 2. Check feasibility of execution of such jobs in fabrication yard.

Department: Iron Making Electrical Maintenance – Coke Plant
What Happened: : On 20th June 2021, the injured person (IP) was going to provide power connection to the belt joint heater fixed at K1A conveyor near CDQ auxiliary building along with his colleague. While moving towards the conveyor, his left foot got twisted due to uneven surface resulting in an injury to his lef
Preliminary Findings: 1. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate. 2. There was uneven surface near the pathway. 3. Housekeeping in the area was well maintained.
Recommendations: 1. Identify Slip/Trip/Fall Hazards and take corrective action for such observations. 2. Refresher training on Slip/Trip/ fall to be given to all employees of Batt#567 -Coke Plant 3. Quality of road repair should be such that there is no uneven surface more than 0.5 inches.

What Happened: : On 23rd June 2021, the injured person (IP) was operating the EOT crane with remote for shifting the gantry portal. During shifting the gantry portal, it struck on his right leg resulting in an injury on his right leg. IP was immediately taken to First Aid Centre and then referred to TMH for furthe
Preliminary Findings: 1. Crane operator was working with remote operated EOT crane. 2. Housekeeping in the area was found adequate. 3. Illumination at the incident site was found adequate. 4. Job was being supervised.
Recommendations: 1. Six directional hazards analysis should be done before starting the job. 2. Correct slinging method should be adopted, keeping in view the weight and centre of gravity of the load.

Injury Description: Crush injury in tip of ring finger and lacerated cut in little finger
What Happened: : Welding activity of 0.423X1355mm width coil with 0.38X900 mm coil was in process at entry section. During indexing, the head end of the coil stuck in the in the gap in support table. While IP was trying to remove the sheet with his hand, the Entry Operator operated the pinch roll no-4 in reverse d
Preliminary Findings: 1. Control stop was not available. 2. Hazard in reverse movement was not identified. 3. IP was using proper PPE’s. Photograph/Diagram:
Recommendations: 1. Use of Auto centering unite for guiding/matching the sheet. 2. Use of tool for guiding/matching sheet during welding till Auto centering unite is not installed. 3. Control stop will be used during use of hand for guiding/matching sheet during ultra-thin gauge welding.

Injury Description: Head injury and fracture in collar bone
What Happened: : On 7th June 2021, the injured person (IP), L1- IRATA Certified technician, was engaged in structural inspection of conveyor trestle of C-24. While inspection, the rope through which he was getting support got entangled with a moving dumper which belongs to M/s UAE. The rope got snapped and the IP
Preliminary Findings: 1. The incident site was not barricaded. 2. Flag man was present at the incident site. 3. Standard operating procedure (MEDM/SI/SOP/04) and Work Permit (110002533849) were found at the site. 4. IP was using all required PPEs.
Recommendations: 1. Only IRATA certified competent vendors to be engaged in rope access jobs. 2. Full road barricading must be done for all rope access jobs over the road. 3. Flag man to be placed 50 m away from work area towards incoming vehicle movement. 4. A pre-start-up check list to be developed & implemented before issue of work permit for rope access jobs.

Incident Type: Dead body of a contract worker found in clarifier tank of Effluent
Injury Description: Details will be available after postmortem
What Happened: : On 03.07.2021, at about 10:50 AM, two contract workers of M/s Murat Construction went to collect the water sample from clarifier tank of Effluent Treatment Plant. After reaching the site, they found one unknown dead body floating on the water near the clarifier bridge. Immediately, clarifier tank
Preliminary Findings: Not Found
Recommendations: Not Found

What Happened: : On 24th May 2021, injured person (IP) was engaged in shifting loose materials from the HSM Lab roof, as a cyclone preparation work. While shifting scaffolding with the help of the other two persons, his left-hand ring finger got pressed by the connecting pipe in scaffolding resulting in an injury.
Preliminary Findings: 1. IP was using leather hand gloves. 2. Illumination at the incident site was adequate.
Recommendations: 1. Material shifting activity will be done under supervision of the supervisor and old materials will be inspected thoroughly before shifting.

Injury Description: Laceration on left hand ring finger
What Happened: : On 8th April 2021, the injured person (IP) was engaged in BC#104 drive changing job of coke circuit. The coupling jaw hub was being disengaged using a wedge and hammer, IP was handling the wedge while another vendor employee was hammering to create gaps. During hammering, a wedge got slipped from
Preliminary Findings: 1. IP was handling the wedge manually. 2. There was long handle wedge available at site, but instead of using this, the IP was using a wedge having no handle. 3. Positive isolation of conveyor drives was ensured. 4. SOP was available for the job at the incident site. 5. The IP was wearing hand gloves and all necessary PPEs. 6. Illumination at the incident site was adequate.
Recommendations: 1. Exploring the standard tackles to disengage the coupling jaw hub for the elimination of hazards associated with the wedge & hammer. 2. SOP to be revised by mentioning the exact tools and tackles to be used for the job. 3. Identify all similar types of practices in the department and eliminate the hazards associated with them in a systematic manner.

Department: Iron Making Elect. Maintenance, Shared Services
What Happened: On July 30, 2024, IP (Injured Person) was going to his residence for lunch on his two-wheeler. At the OHS island, a car from Digi Wheels approached from the petrol pump side and accidently collided with the two-wheeler. As a result, the IP fell on the road and sustained injury to his left ankle. He was taken to the West Plant First Aid for
Preliminary Findings: 1. Both the car and the two-wheeler did not slow down while approaching at the island as captured in CCTV.
Recommendations: 1. One must ensure to conduct regular basic checks and maintenance practices to ensure their vehicles are in good working condition. 2. Departments to ensure imparting defensive driving training to employees to enhance road safety.

Injury Description: Multiple fracture in right leg ankle
What Happened: : On May 06, 2024, the operations team reported an issue with 80/20T Crane-1 main hoist. The mechanical maintenance team investigated and found that the crane wire rope was overlapped on the drum. To address this, the main hoist of the crane was lowered and raised simultaneously. IP (Injured Person)
Preliminary Findings: 1. IP was wearing all the required personal protective equipment (PPE). 2. The wire rope was overlapped on drum, so lowering and hoisting was done to reposition the rope on groove. 3. Rotating part of the Tacho coupling, and shaft was unguarded.
Recommendations: 1. Immediately, guards should be installed on all rotating parts. 2. Unwanted material should be removed from crane gantry. 3. All the crane gantry of the entire plant should be audited to deploy the recommendations.

Injury Description: Fracture in right wrist radius bone
What Happened: : On May 12, 2024, IP (Injured Person) was in B-shift. He was engaged in assembly work. During assembling, IP moved his sitting stool to match the center bolt. After aligning the hole, he forgot to return the stool to its proper position and attempted to sit, causing imbalance. Subsequently, he fell
Preliminary Findings: 1. The incident occurred because of the lack of awareness or oversight regarding the position of the sitting stool after it was moved.
Recommendations: 1. Adjustable arrangement should be provided for either equipment. 2. Study the ergonomic way of working postures at Roll assembly area.

What Happened: : At HR Slitter, the edge trimming is fixed into the scrap winder which passes through the dispenser roll. Sometimes during trimming, the edge trimming breaks and comes out from the scrap winder. In such a situation, the line is stopped, the emergency stop for scrap winder is applied and then it is
Preliminary Findings: 1. Scrap winder emergency stop was operated as per the process. 2. Scrap winder fixing job is a manual job. 3. Standard operating procedure was found inadequate- abnormal condition of broken trimming edge fixing job was not addressed. 4. There was a communication gap between the helper and the operator.
Recommendations: 1. Limit switch should be installed in existing entry gate. 2. Lock and key based emergency stop should be implemented. 3. RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) based access control should be implemented.

What Happened: : On May 03, 2024, a Self-Loading Concrete Mixer (SLM) was placed behind the newly developed vendor park at the Khondbond Iron Mine (KIM) Wet Plant to fill water from a nearby PVC (Poly Vinyl Chloride) water tank. Meanwhile, while accelerating the engine of the SLM to gain the required Rotation Per
Preliminary Findings: 1. Water filling in SLM was done in an undesignated place. 2. Only one wheel chock was applied behind one of the wheels. This could only prevent backward movement of vehicle. 3. Parking brake was not applied. 4. The vehicle was kept in forward gear condition.
Recommendations: 1. Parking brake and wheel chock must be applied adequately in heavy vehicles for any enabling activity or during the waiting period. 2. Ensure barricading around such vehicles for access control.

What Happened: : On May 01, 2024, IP (Injured Person) was on his way to plant for general shift duty. He stopped his bike near MPO building, to punch his RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) for attendance. While parking, the side stand of his bike broke, and the bike fell over him. As a result, he sustained an i
Preliminary Findings: 1. Bike side stand was found damaged and broken. 2. Illumination of the area was found ok. 3. Incident site ground surface was found ok.
Recommendations: 1. All employees should check the condition of their vehicle parts and safety accessories. 2. Employee should be mindful when parking their two-wheelers on stands, as there is a risk of the vehicle falling or tipping over.

Injury Description: Fracture in left hand middle finger
What Happened: On April 30, 2024, IP (Injured Person) was deployed in OCRM workshop. He was working for CRM- 5 hoist rope drum machining work, along with the operator. After positioning the drum, IP was removing the centre plate, but it got slipped during removal. Using his hands, he tried to prevent the plate from falling. This led to injury in his left-hand middle finger. He was taken to OHC (Occupational Health Centre) for
Preliminary Findings: 1. IP was wearing proper PPEs. 2. Illumination at the site was found ok.
Recommendations: 1. Awareness training on manual material handling should be re-imparted to all concerned workers. 2. Safer and standard practices should be explored and deployed at workshop machineries.

What Happened: Not Found
Preliminary Findings: Not Found
Recommendations: Not Found

What Happened: On April 1, 2024, IP (Injured Person) was in C-shift. While he was going towards inspection area from Pay Off Reel Pulpit, he stepped on the plastic chain (used for barricading). Due to lack of alertness, he slipped, and his ankle got twisted. He was taken to First Aid Centre. After
Preliminary Findings: 1. Housekeeping and illumination of the area was found good. 2. After folding back the chain, it was kept on one side of the floor. 3. IP inadvertently stepped on plastic chain, resulting in ankle twist.
Recommendations: 1. A designated hook should be provided to put the chain back. 2. People should be sensitized on behavioural safety with appropriate communication for optimal impact.

What Happened: : On April 7, 2024, IP (Injured Person) was trying to pluck tamarind from a tree standing on an empty equipment box cover (box height was 2.4 meter approx.). During this, the box top cover collapsed, which led to him falling inside the box. He was taken out of the box from one side by his co-workers
Preliminary Findings: 1. IP had informed his co-worker that he was going for the nature’s call before the incident. 2. IP was deployed for stacking/shifting of scaffolding materials at a site 400 meters away from the incident location.
Recommendations: 1. Removing fruits from fruit bearing trees within the plant premises should be part of routine maintenance and upkeep practices. 2. Widespread communication regarding the prohibition of climbing on trees and fruits plucking from trees within the plant premises should be ensured.

What Happened: : On April 17, 2024, a team of 11 workmen were assigned to carry out conveyor gallery assembly work at the MHS area. While installing a seal plate (length of seal plate measures 10 meters and weighs approximately 200 kg.), it was being inserted into the gallery using F15 Crane. During this process,
Preliminary Findings: 1. All workmen were wearing all required PPEs. 2. The used seal plate design (with respect to location) was different from all other galleries. 3. The used seal plate was of 10 metre length and was being inserted using crowbar as well as by pushing it by hand from below in forward direction.
Recommendations: 1. For safe erection of seal plate, it should have been cut in small pieces, and then should have been inserted and joint by welding.

Injury Description: Crush injury in left hand ring finger
What Happened: : On April 25, 2024, IP (Injured Person) was engaged in old PCI mill pull rod cylinder replacement job at H-BF. The cylinder was being handled using chain block and wire rope sling at the working area. Next to the cylinder, there was a platform, where the injured person placed his left hand. Unfortu
Preliminary Findings: 1. All workmen were wearing all the required PPEs. 2. E- permit was taken for the job. 3. Standard operating procedure was available at the site. 4. The cylinder was lifted in the wrong orientation.
Recommendations: 1. Cylinder should be lifted in the same orientation as required in final assembly. 2. Only the required number of manpower should be present during the job execution.

Department: Ductile Iron Pipe plant (Metaliks Division; erstwhile Tata

What Happened: : Injured Person (IP) was engaged in Mill inspection job of field devices (during night shift of 1st June’22) at Mill Exit area of PLTCM during planned shutdown. Behaviour based training should be given to all, to bring a change in individual behaviour.

Preliminary Findings: 1. Standard Operating Procedure for the job was available and was being followed. 2. Illumination was found appropriate. 3. Play in the BOB hinge pin was observed along with accumulation of dirt in area left over by cooling water.
Recommendations: 1. Locking arrangement should be provided for breakout box to avoid free fall of its upper portion. 2. Scheduled cleaning should be done to avoid restrictions in the movement of breakout boxes due to dirt deposits.

Preliminary Findings: 1. A small hole was found in the corner of the floor beside the food storage area. 2. Illumination in the room was not sufficient. 3. Bush was grown outside the boundary wall of canteen area.
Recommendations: 1. Regular Carbolic acid spray is to be ensured in such areas. 2. Any small hole or rodent access found in the canteen and other respective locations should be sealed. 3. Area outside the office should be cleaned properly along with regular bush cutting.

What Happened: On May 24, 2023, IP (Injured Person) was going to designated rest shed. He tried to take a short cut for rest room. What Happened: : On May 3, 2023, while onboard a shuttle bus-03 from blast furnace, IP (Injured Person) attempted to disembark approximately 30-meter before reaching the designated bus stop near the administrative building. Recommendations: 1. Hydraulic doors of all Shuttle buses should be ensured operative and administered to closed/ locked when the bus is moving. 2. Both hands should be free, and a proper handrail should be used while ascending or descending from a bus.

Preliminary Findings: 1. Standard Operating Procedure, HIRA (Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment), TBT (Toolbox Talk) & JHA (Job Hazard Analysis) were available at site. 2. Work permit and height permit were available at site. 3. IP was wearing all mandatory PPEs.

Preliminary Findings: 1. Staircase was free from slip/trip/fall hazard. 2. Handrail was provided at one side of the staircase. 3. IP has 13 years of experience in Tata Steel Meramandali, and more than 17 years of overall working experience in the similar field.

What Happened: : On April 24, 2023, IP (Injured person) was in General shift duty. He was attending a breakdown in KN212 4A (ball bearing outer race-track-grinding machine). During troubleshooting, the IP was observing for oil leakage from loading cylinder piston rod, which was standstill in extended position, by
Preliminary Findings: 1. IP was wearing all the mandatory PPEs (Personal Protective equipment). 2. SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) was available. ESI (Energy Source Identification) document was available, and it mentioned about hydraulic as an energy source. 3. Despite the SOP clearly stating that visual checks should only be performed on a running machine, an inspection was conducted by physically touching the machine, which is the violation of SOP.
Recommendations: 1. SOP should be revised by mentioning the positive isolation of all energy sources according to ESI document. 2. Control-stop procedure should be incorporated in SOP for maintenance, inspection including visual check in manual/auto mode of machine. 3. The ESI should be amended accordingly, and the maintenance team should be trained.
